task_completion.description: ESA SNAP Desktop is open and the Landsat GeoTIFF is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SNAP Desktop application window is visible. In the 'Product Explorer' pane, a product corresponding to the Landsat multispectral TIFF (e.g., landsat_multispectral) is listed.

task_completion.description: The RGB image configuration dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Select RGB-Image Channels' (or similar) is visible on the screen, showing dropdowns or lists for selecting Red, Green, and Blue channels.

task_completion.description: The Red channel is assigned to band_2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the RGB configuration dialog, the Red channel dropdown or selection explicitly shows 'band_2'.

task_completion.description: The Green channel is assigned to band_3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the RGB configuration dialog, the Green channel dropdown or selection explicitly shows 'band_3'.

task_completion.description: The Blue channel is assigned to band_4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the RGB configuration dialog, the Blue channel dropdown or selection explicitly shows 'band_4'.

task_completion.description: The false-color RGB composite is created and displayed in the main view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new image view window is open in the main SNAP workspace displaying the rendered RGB image, typically with a tab title indicating it is an RGB composite of the loaded product.

integrity.description: The agent used the SNAP Desktop GUI to perform the task rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with SNAP menus (e.g., right-clicking the product or using the Window menu to open the RGB dialog) and dialog boxes, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts (like snappy) being executed.

integrity.description: The RGB composite is displayed genuinely within the SNAP Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rendered image appears inside the SNAP internal window system with standard SNAP toolbars, navigation tabs, and panels surrounding it, not in an external image viewer or web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not edit text files or rename bands to fake the task completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The band assignments are made through the standard SNAP dropdown menus. There is no evidence of text editors or terminal windows being used to alter product metadata files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is '/home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif' containing 4 bands. The agent must use the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI. Crucially, the agent must assign the RGB channels exactly as instructed: Red = band_2, Green = band_3, Blue = band_4. The VLM should look for these exact string matches in the RGB channel selection dialog, regardless of the actual physical wavelengths those bands represent.
